summary: Tune AWS Backup plans and coverage: adjust a plan's schedule (cron is UTC) and retention, add an extra backup rule, onboard resources with a tag-based selection, detach a selection, and tear down a vault (engineer-only, unrecoverable). Not set up yet? Start with Create a backup vault, then Create a backup plan, then add a selection — after that every operation here has something to target. On-demand backup jobs are excluded by design: running a job now is an operational action, not a configuration change.

operations.title: Change a backup plan rule's schedule
operations.summary: Change the time this backup rule runs. Schedules are in UTC, so convert from your local time. No downtime; only future backups shift.
operations.description: Retune the cron schedule of an existing backup rule (e.g. move a daily job earlier to clear the batch window). In-place update to the plan; no workload downtime and no effect on existing recovery points — only when future jobs fire changes. AWS Backup cron is evaluated in UTC, so the requester must offset it from their local time.

params.name: plan
params.label: Backup plan
params.help: The aws_backup_plan whose rule you want to reschedule.

params.name: rule_name
params.label: Rule name
params.help: Which rule inside the plan to modify. Leave as the plan's primary rule unless it has multiple.

params.name: new_schedule
params.label: New schedule (cron, UTC)
params.help: AWS Backup cron expression in UTC. Example: cron(0 17 ? * * *) = daily 17:00 UTC.

operations.title: Change a backup plan rule's retention
operations.summary: Change how long this rule's backups are kept. Shortening it can permanently delete existing backups early; a retention floor is enforced.
operations.description: Adjust how many days recovery points from a rule are retained before automatic expiry. Extending retention is safe and additive; SHORTENING it can cause AWS Backup to permanently expire existing recovery points at the next lifecycle pass — that data cannot be recovered. In-place plan update, no downtime, but data-protection relevant, so it carries a guardrail and a floor on the minimum retention.

params.name: plan
params.label: Backup plan
params.help: The aws_backup_plan whose rule retention you want to change.

params.name: rule_name
params.label: Rule name
params.help: Which rule inside the plan to modify.

params.name: new_delete_after_days
params.label: New retention (days)
params.help: Days to retain recovery points before expiry. Must exceed any cold_storage_after by at least 90 days per AWS rules. Shortening below the current value may permanently delete existing recovery points.

operations.title: Add a backup rule to an existing plan
operations.summary: Add another schedule to this plan, like a monthly rule alongside the daily one. Adds ongoing storage cost that grows with how often it runs.
operations.description: Append an additional rule to an existing plan — for example a long-retention monthly rule alongside the existing daily one — targeting a chosen vault with its own schedule and retention. In-place plan update, no downtime. Creates ongoing storage cost proportional to schedule frequency times retention, so it is guardrailed.

params.name: plan
params.label: Backup plan
params.help: The aws_backup_plan to append a new rule to.

params.name: rule_name
params.label: New rule name
params.help: Unique name for the new rule within this plan.

params.name: target_vault_name
params.label: Target vault
params.help: Vault the new rule writes recovery points into.

params.name: schedule
params.label: Schedule (cron, UTC)
params.help: Cron in UTC. Example: cron(0 18 1 * ? *) = 1st of month 18:00 UTC.

params.name: delete_after_days
params.label: Retention (days)
params.help: Days to retain recovery points created by this rule before expiry.

operations.title: Add a tag-based resource selection to a plan
operations.summary: Start backing up resources that match a tag you pick. Adds new backup storage cost and protects them on the plan's schedule.
operations.description: Onboard resources into a backup plan by creating a new aws_backup_selection that matches a tag key/value (e.g. Backup=daily on EBS volumes and EFS). Everything matching the tag begins backing up on the plan's schedule, creating new storage cost. No downtime on the workload. The AWS Backup service role is picked from the account inventory — the requester never supplies a raw role by hand.

params.name: plan
params.label: Backup plan
params.help: The aws_backup_plan the new selection attaches to.

params.name: selection_name
params.label: Selection name
params.help: Unique name for the new selection resource.

params.name: backup_tag_key
params.label: Match tag key
params.help: Tag key resources must carry to be included (e.g. Backup).

params.name: backup_tag_value
params.label: Match tag value
params.help: Tag value resources must carry (e.g. daily).

params.name: iam_role_arn
params.label: Backup service role
params.help: IAM role AWS Backup assumes to snapshot the selected resources; must grant the AWSBackupServiceRolePolicy (or equivalent). Only roles managed in this account's Terraform appear — a console-created backup service role must be adopted into the estate before it shows up here.

decisions: Confirm the service role scope if the non-default role is chosen

operations.title: Remove a resource selection from a plan
operations.summary: Stop future backups for resources this selection covers. Existing backups are kept. A second approver reviews it before this takes effect.
operations.description: Detach an aws_backup_selection so the resources it matched stop being backed up on future runs. Existing recovery points already in the vault are NOT deleted and remain restorable until their retention expires, so this is reversible by re-adding the selection. No workload downtime, but it silently drops resources from protection, so it is guardrailed with a second approver.

params.name: selection
params.label: Resource selection
params.help: The aws_backup_selection to remove. Future backups of its matched resources stop; existing recovery points are retained.

operations.title: Delete a backup vault
operations.summary: Permanently delete this empty backup vault. Every backup it ever held is already gone by this point; an engineer reviews it first.
operations.description: Destroy an aws_backup_vault. AWS refuses to delete a vault that still holds recovery points, so this only succeeds once the vault is empty — at which point every recovery point that ever lived in it is already gone. This is the destructive, unrecoverable teardown that stands in for deleting recovery points: no restore is possible afterward. Engineer-authored and engineer-reviewed only.

params.name: vault
params.label: Backup vault
params.help: The aws_backup_vault to destroy. Must be empty; any recovery points are permanently unrecoverable once removed.

operations.title: Toggle vault force-destroy
operations.summary: Allow or block a future vault deletion from also wiping out its backups. Removes a safety net when turned on; it deletes nothing by itself.
operations.description: Enable or disable force_destroy on a backup vault. When true, a future terraform destroy will also delete all recovery points held in the vault. Updates in place, reversible, no downtime; it deletes nothing on its own but toggling it on removes a guardrail against accidental data loss, so it runs with guardrails.

params.name: vault
params.label: Backup vault
params.help: The backup vault whose force-destroy flag you are toggling, picked from Terraform-managed vaults so this data-loss guardrail can only be changed on a known vault.

params.name: force_destroy
params.label: Force destroy on delete
params.help: true lets a future destroy remove all recovery points in this vault; false protects them.

operations.title: Add a tag to the vault
operations.summary: Add or update a tag on this backup vault. Metadata only, no effect on how backups run.
operations.description: Add or overwrite a single key/value tag on a backup vault. Cosmetic metadata change, updates in place, fully reversible, no downtime.

params.name: vault
params.label: Backup vault
params.help: The backup vault receiving the tag, selected from Terraform inventory so the metadata is applied to a known vault.

params.name: tag_key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: 1-128 chars: letters, digits and + - = . _ : / @ space.

params.name: tag_value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: Up to 256 chars: letters, digits and + - = . _ : / @ space.

operations.title: Remove a tag from the vault
operations.summary: Remove a tag from this backup vault. Metadata only, no effect on how backups run.
operations.description: Remove a single tag (by key) from a backup vault. Cosmetic metadata change, updates in place, reversible by re-adding, no downtime.

params.name: vault
params.label: Backup vault
params.help: The backup vault to remove the tag from, selected from Terraform inventory so only a managed vault's metadata changes.

params.name: tag_key
params.label: Tag key to remove
params.help: Exact key of the tag to remove, 1-128 chars.

operations.title: Request another change to Backup vault
operations.summary: Request any other change to this backup vault, such as its encryption key or its name; an engineer writes and reviews the Terraform.
operations.description: Catch-all for any backup vault change not offered as a bounded self-service form (for example changing the KMS key or renaming, both of which force replacement). An engineer authors and reviews the Terraform.

params.name: vault
params.label: Backup vault
params.help: The backup vault this request is about, when the estate already tracks one. Leave blank when describing one that is not tracked yet.

params.name: purpose
params.label: What change do you need?
params.help: Anything not offered as a bounded form — an engineer authors the Terraform.

operations.title: Change IAM role for backup selection
operations.summary: Change the IAM role a backup selection uses. It gets recreated to apply this; existing backups stay untouched. An engineer reviews it.
operations.description: Update iam_role_arn — the IAM role AWS Backup assumes to back up the selected resources. Chosen from existing IAM roles (inventory-bounded), so no free-form input. AWS Backup selections are immutable, so the selection is destroyed and recreated to apply this; existing recovery points in the vault are untouched, and it is reversible. A role lacking the required backup permissions will cause backup jobs to fail, so this is guardrailed rather than auto-applied.

params.name: selection
params.label: Backup selection
params.help: Which backup selection to modify.

params.name: iam_role_arn
params.label: IAM role
params.help: Role AWS Backup assumes; must grant the AWSBackupServiceRolePolicy (or equivalent) for the selected resource types.

operations.title: Add tag-based rule to backup selection
operations.summary: Add a tag rule to cover more resources in this selection. It gets recreated to apply; existing backups stay untouched. Engineer-reviewed.
operations.description: Append a selection_tag block so AWS Backup includes any resource whose tag matches the given key/value. selection_tag supports the STRINGEQUALS operator only. Expands or refines what the plan backs up. The selection is destroyed and recreated to apply this; existing recovery points in the vault are untouched, and it is reversible. Changes protection scope, so it is guardrailed.

params.name: selection
params.label: Backup selection
params.help: Which backup selection to modify.

params.name: type
params.label: Match operator
params.help: selection_tag supports STRINGEQUALS only.

params.name: key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: AWS tag key to match on (1-128 chars).

params.name: value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: Exact AWS tag value to match (0-256 chars).

operations.title: Remove a tag-based rule from backup selection
operations.summary: Remove a tag rule to cover fewer resources in this selection. It's recreated to apply; existing backups stay untouched. Engineer-reviewed.
operations.description: Remove a selection_tag block identified by its operator/key/value. Resources matched only by that tag will no longer be included by the selection, reducing backup scope, so this is guardrailed. The selection is destroyed and recreated to apply this; existing recovery points in the vault are untouched. Reversible by re-adding the block.

params.name: selection
params.label: Backup selection
params.help: Which backup selection to modify.

params.name: type
params.label: Match operator
params.help: Operator of the selection_tag block to remove.

params.name: key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: Tag key of the selection_tag block to remove.

params.name: value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: Tag value of the selection_tag block to remove.

operations.title: Add tag condition to backup selection
operations.summary: Add a filter that narrows which tagged resources this selection covers. No downtime, and it is reversible.
operations.description: Append a condition sub-block (string_equals, string_not_equals, string_like, or string_not_like) to refine which tagged resources the selection matches. The *_like operators accept '*' wildcards. In-place update, reversible, no downtime; changes protection scope so it is guardrailed.

params.name: selection
params.label: Backup selection
params.help: Which backup selection to modify.

params.name: operator
params.label: Condition operator
params.help: Comparator sub-block to add.

params.name: key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: AWS tag key the condition matches on.

params.name: value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: Tag value to match; '*' wildcards allowed for the *_like operators.

operations.title: Remove a tag condition from backup selection
operations.summary: Remove a filter from this selection, which can widen what it covers. It's recreated to apply this; an engineer reviews it.
operations.description: Remove a condition sub-block identified by its operator/key/value. Relaxes the tag filter, which broadens or otherwise changes which resources the selection matches, so this is guardrailed. The selection is destroyed and recreated to apply this; existing recovery points in the vault are untouched. Reversible by re-adding the condition.

params.name: selection
params.label: Backup selection
params.help: Which backup selection to modify.

params.name: operator
params.label: Condition operator
params.help: Comparator sub-block to remove.

params.name: key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: Tag key of the condition block to remove.

params.name: value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: Tag value of the condition block to remove.

operations.title: Request another change to backup selection
operations.summary: Request any other change to this backup selection, such as its region or resource list; an engineer writes and reviews the Terraform.
operations.description: Catch-all for any backup selection change not offered as a bounded form above — for example changing the resource region, editing the resources/not_resources ARN lists in bulk, or any ForceNew change (name, plan_id). An engineer authors and reviews the Terraform.

params.name: selection
params.label: Resource selection
params.help: The backup selection this request is about, when the estate already tracks one. Leave blank when describing one that is not tracked yet.

params.name: purpose
params.label: What change do you need?
params.help: Anything not offered as a bounded form — an engineer authors the Terraform.

operations.title: Rename backup plan
operations.summary: Rename this backup plan. This REPLACES it with a new ID and recreates its selections; vault backups are untouched. Reviewed first.
operations.description: Rename the plan. CAUTION: `name` is ForceNew on aws_backup_plan — Terraform REPLACES the plan (new plan ID and ARN), and aws_backup_selection associations referencing it are recreated with it. Existing recovery points are unaffected (they live in the vault) and schedules resume under the replacement plan. The -/+ replace is visible in the diff at review; approval required.

params.name: plan
params.label: Backup plan
params.help: The backup plan to rename, chosen from Terraform-managed plans. Warning: name is ForceNew — the plan is replaced (new ID/ARN) and its selections are recreated; existing recovery points in the vault are kept.

params.name: new_name
params.label: New plan name
params.help: 1-50 chars: letters, digits, hyphen, underscore, period.

operations.downtime: none (plan + selections replaced; recovery points and vault untouched)

operations.title: Toggle continuous backup (PITR) on a rule
operations.summary: Turn continuous backup (point-in-time recovery) on or off for supported resources like S3 and RDS. No downtime; reviewed before it applies.
operations.description: Enable or disable `enable_continuous_backup` on a rule, controlling point-in-time recovery for supported resources (S3, RDS). Reversible, no downtime. Continuous backup is incompatible with a cold-storage lifecycle on the same rule, so the plan is reviewed before apply.

params.name: plan
params.label: Backup plan
params.help: The backup plan containing the rule to change, picked from Terraform inventory so the point-in-time-recovery toggle applies to a known plan.

params.name: rule_name
params.label: Rule name
params.help: Name of the existing rule within the plan to modify.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Continuous backup
params.help: true turns on continuous backup (point-in-time recovery) for supported resources such as S3 and RDS on this rule, false returns it to snapshot-only backups; the allowlist permits nothing besides these two values.

operations.title: Change backup start window (minutes)
operations.summary: Change how long a backup job can wait to start before it's cancelled. Reversible, no downtime.
operations.description: Set `start_window` on a rule — minutes AWS Backup waits for a job to start before canceling it. Bounded 60-1440. Must stay at least 60 minutes below completion_window; the plan is reviewed before apply. Reversible, no downtime.

params.name: plan
params.label: Backup plan
params.help: The backup plan containing the rule whose start window you are changing, selected from Terraform-managed plans so the timing change lands on a known plan.

params.name: rule_name
params.label: Rule name
params.help: Name of the existing rule within the plan to modify.

params.name: minutes
params.label: Start window (minutes)
params.help: Minimum 60. Must be at least 60 below the completion window.

operations.title: Change backup completion window (minutes)
operations.summary: Change how long a backup job is allowed to run before it's cancelled. Reversible, no downtime.
operations.description: Set `completion_window` on a rule — maximum minutes a backup job may run before AWS Backup cancels it. Bounded 120-2880. Must be at least 60 minutes greater than start_window; the plan is reviewed before apply. Reversible, no downtime.

params.name: plan
params.label: Backup plan
params.help: The backup plan containing the rule whose completion window you are changing, selected from Terraform-managed plans so the timing change lands on a known plan.

params.name: rule_name
params.label: Rule name
params.help: Name of the existing rule within the plan to modify.

params.name: minutes
params.label: Completion window (minutes)
params.help: Minimum 120. Must exceed the start window by at least 60 minutes.

operations.title: Toggle archive-tier opt-in on a rule
operations.summary: Let eligible backups move into lower-cost archive storage over time, or keep them out. No downtime.
operations.description: Enable or disable `lifecycle.opt_in_to_archive_for_supported_resources` on a rule, allowing supported recovery points to transition to the archive storage tier per the rule's lifecycle settings. Reversible, no downtime.

params.name: plan
params.label: Backup plan
params.help: The backup plan containing the rule to change, chosen from Terraform inventory so the archive-tier toggle targets a known plan.

params.name: rule_name
params.label: Rule name
params.help: Name of the existing rule within the plan to modify.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Archive-tier opt-in
params.help: true lets supported recovery points transition to the lower-cost archive storage tier according to the rule's lifecycle settings, false keeps them in warm storage; only true or false is accepted.

operations.title: Change schedule timezone on a rule
operations.summary: Change the timezone this rule's schedule runs on, shifting when it actually fires. No downtime.
operations.description: Set `schedule_expression_timezone` — the IANA timezone the rule's cron schedule is evaluated in — from a bounded list. Shifts when backup windows fire (the cron expression itself is unchanged). Reversible, no downtime.

params.name: plan
params.label: Backup plan
params.help: The backup plan containing the rule whose schedule timezone you are changing, selected from Terraform-managed plans so the shift in backup timing applies to a known plan.

params.name: rule_name
params.label: Rule name
params.help: Name of the existing rule within the plan to modify.

params.name: timezone
params.label: Timezone
params.help: IANA timezone name.

operations.title: Add or update a tag
operations.summary: Add or update a tag on this backup plan. Metadata only, no effect on how backups run.
operations.description: Add or overwrite a single entry in the plan's `tags` map. Cosmetic metadata; reversible with no downtime.

params.name: plan
params.label: Backup plan
params.help: The backup plan receiving the tag entry, picked from Terraform inventory so the metadata lands on a known plan.

params.name: key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: 1-128 chars from the AWS tag key character set.

params.name: value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: 0-256 chars from the AWS tag value character set.

operations.title: Remove a tag
operations.summary: Remove a tag from this backup plan. Metadata only, no effect on how backups run.
operations.description: Remove a single entry from the plan's `tags` map by key. Reversible with no downtime.

params.name: plan
params.label: Backup plan
params.help: The backup plan to remove the tag entry from, picked from Terraform inventory so only a managed plan's metadata changes.

params.name: key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: Exact key of the tag entry to remove.

operations.title: Request another change to backup plan
operations.summary: Request any other change to this backup plan, like cross-region copies or advanced settings; an engineer writes and reviews the Terraform.
operations.description: Catch-all for any aws_backup_plan change not offered as a bounded form (e.g. copy_action cross-region/cross-account copies, advanced_backup_setting WindowsVSS, scan_setting/GuardDuty malware scanning, target_vault_name changes, or renaming a rule). An engineer authors and reviews the Terraform.

params.name: plan
params.label: Backup plan
params.help: The backup plan this request is about, when the estate already tracks one. Leave blank when describing one that is not tracked yet.

params.name: purpose
params.label: What change do you need?
params.help: Anything not offered as a bounded form — an engineer authors the Terraform.

operations.title: Rename a backup rule
operations.summary: Rename a rule inside this backup plan. Doesn't change its schedule or existing backups; update any automation that references the old name.
operations.description: Update the `rule_name` (display name) of an existing rule within a backup plan, applied in place via UpdateBackupPlan. Does not change the plan, its schedule/retention, or any existing recovery points. Reversible with no downtime. Automation or drift checks that reference the rule by name may need updating, so like the plan-rename op it is not auto-applied.

params.name: plan
params.label: Backup plan
params.help: The aws_backup_plan containing the rule to rename.

params.name: rule_name
params.label: Rule name
params.help: Current name of the rule to rename. 1-50 chars: letters, digits, hyphen, underscore, period.

params.name: new_rule_name
params.label: New rule name
params.help: New rule name. 1-50 chars: letters, digits, hyphen, underscore, period.

operations.title: Change a rule's target vault
operations.summary: Send this rule's future backups to a different vault. Backups already stored in the old vault stay there.
operations.description: Update `target_vault_name` on an existing rule so future recovery points are written to a different vault, chosen from inventory. Existing recovery points already in the old vault are not moved or deleted. In-place plan update, reversible, no downtime; it changes backup topology and which vault's protections apply going forward, so it is guardrailed.

params.name: plan
params.label: Backup plan
params.help: The aws_backup_plan whose rule you want to retarget.

params.name: rule_name
params.label: Rule name
params.help: Name of the existing rule within the plan to modify.

params.name: target_vault_name
params.label: Target vault
params.help: Vault the rule's future recovery points are written into.

operations.title: Add or update a recovery-point tag on a rule
operations.summary: Add or update a tag applied to backups this rule creates from now on. Metadata only, no effect on how backups run.
operations.description: Add or overwrite a single key/value entry in a rule's `recovery_point_tags` map — metadata applied to recovery points the rule creates. Cosmetic metadata; in-place plan update, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: plan
params.label: Backup plan
params.help: The aws_backup_plan containing the rule.

params.name: rule_name
params.label: Rule name
params.help: Name of the existing rule within the plan whose recovery-point tags you are editing.

params.name: key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: 1-128 chars from the AWS tag key character set.

params.name: value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: 0-256 chars from the AWS tag value character set.

operations.title: Remove a recovery-point tag from a rule
operations.summary: Remove a tag applied to backups this rule creates from now on. Metadata only, no effect on how backups run.
operations.description: Remove a single entry (by key) from a rule's `recovery_point_tags` map. Cosmetic metadata; in-place plan update, reversible by re-adding, no downtime.

params.name: plan
params.label: Backup plan
params.help: The aws_backup_plan containing the rule.

params.name: rule_name
params.label: Rule name
params.help: Name of the existing rule within the plan whose recovery-point tags you are editing.

params.name: key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: Exact key of the recovery-point tag entry to remove.

operations.title: Set a rule's malware scan action
operations.summary: Set how this rule scans backups for malware. Choosing the faster scan mode checks less thoroughly.
operations.description: Configure the `scan_action` block on a rule — the malware scanner and scan depth. `malware_scanner` is fixed to the only currently supported value `GUARDDUTY`; `scan_mode` selects `FULL_SCAN` or `INCREMENTAL_SCAN`. In-place plan update, reversible, no downtime. Choosing INCREMENTAL_SCAN silently reduces detection depth versus FULL_SCAN without breaking backups, so it is guardrailed.

params.name: plan
params.label: Backup plan
params.help: The aws_backup_plan containing the rule.

params.name: rule_name
params.label: Rule name
params.help: Name of the existing rule within the plan to modify.

params.name: malware_scanner
params.label: Malware scanner
params.help: Only GUARDDUTY is currently supported.

params.name: scan_mode
params.label: Scan mode
params.help: FULL_SCAN scans all data; INCREMENTAL_SCAN scans only changes and can miss threats a full scan would catch.

operations.title: Set the plan's malware scan setting
operations.summary: Set which resource types this plan scans for malware and who runs it. A narrower list means less coverage.
operations.description: Configure the plan-wide `scan_setting` block — the malware scanner, the resource types scanned, and the scanner IAM role (chosen from inventory). `malware_scanner` is fixed to `GUARDDUTY`; `resource_types` is any of `EBS`, `EC2`, `S3`, `ALL`. In-place plan update, reversible, no downtime. Narrowing resource_types silently reduces scan coverage without breaking backups, so it is guardrailed.

params.name: plan
params.label: Backup plan
params.help: The aws_backup_plan whose scan_setting you want to configure.

params.name: malware_scanner
params.label: Malware scanner
params.help: Only GUARDDUTY is currently supported.

params.name: resource_types
params.label: Resource types
params.help: Resource types scanned for malware. Each member must be one of EBS, EC2, S3, ALL.

params.name: scanner_role_arn
params.label: Scanner IAM role
params.help: IAM role AWS Backup assumes to scan resources; an under-permissioned role causes scan failures rather than a new exposure.

operations.title: Reassign a backup selection to a different plan
operations.summary: Move this selection's resources onto a different backup plan. It's recreated to apply; existing backups stay untouched. Engineer-reviewed.
operations.description: Change `plan_id` — the aws_backup_plan an existing aws_backup_selection is associated with — moving the selection's matched resources onto a different plan's schedule and target vault. The new plan is chosen from inventory (existing aws_backup_plan resources), so there is no free-form input. AWS Backup selections are immutable in the provider, so Terraform recreates the selection to re-associate it (a destroy-and-create of this membership resource, not of the protected workload); existing recovery points already in the vault are NOT deleted and the workload is untouched. Reversible by pointing plan_id back at the original plan. Guardrailed with a second approver because it changes which plan protects the resources.

params.name: selection
params.label: Backup selection
params.help: The aws_backup_selection to re-associate with a different plan.

params.name: new_plan
params.label: New backup plan
params.help: The aws_backup_plan to associate the selection with, chosen from existing plans. The selection is recreated under this plan; recovery points already in the vault are unaffected.

operations.title: Create a backup vault
operations.summary: Request a new backup vault to store recovery points. An engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: The first step of setting up backups: the safe recovery points are stored in. After the vault, create a plan, then a selection. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish.

params.name: vault_name
params.label: Vault name
params.help: The safe recovery points are stored in, like daily-backups.

params.name: kms_key
params.label: Encryption key
params.help: Only change for key-scoped compliance needs; blank uses the managed backup key.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Vault name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this vault holds
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this vault.

decisions: Vault lock and compliance retention are a separate engineer decision

operations.title: Create a backup plan
operations.summary: Request a new backup plan with a first daily schedule. An engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: The second step of setting up backups: the schedule-and-retention policy, starting with one daily rule. Next step after the plan exists: add a selection so resources start backing up. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish.

params.name: plan_name
params.label: Plan name
params.help: The schedule-and-retention policy, like standard-retention.

params.name: rule_name
params.label: First rule name
params.help: Plans hold rules; start with one.

params.name: vault
params.label: Target vault
params.help: Where this rule's recovery points land. Create a vault first if the list is empty.

params.name: schedule
params.label: Schedule (cron, UTC)
params.help: Cron in UTC. The default, cron(0 17 ? * * *), is daily 17:00 UTC.

params.name: retention_days
params.label: Keep recovery points (days)
params.help: How long before recovery points expire.

params.name: start_window_minutes
params.label: Start window (minutes)
params.help: How long a job may wait to start before it is marked failed.

params.name: completion_window_minutes
params.label: Completion window (minutes)
params.help: How long a job may run.

params.name: continuous_backup
params.label: Point-in-time recovery?
params.help: For supported services, enables restore to any moment inside retention.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Plan name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this plan holds
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this plan.

decisions: Add a second monthly or long-retention rule after creation with the add-a-backup-rule action
Cross-region copy is an engineer decision
